title: Institutional Credit Management (ICM) Product Developer - Artificial Intelligence (AI), Vice President
company_focus: Global banking and financial services institution
description:
Whether you’re at the start of your career or looking to
discover your next adventure, your story begins here.  At
Citi, you’ll have the opportunity to expand your skills and
make a difference at one of the world’s most global banks.
We’re fully committed to supporting your growth and
development from the start with extensive on-the-job
training and exposure to senior leaders, as well as more
traditional learning.  You’ll also have the chance to give
back and make a positive impact on where we live and work
through volunteerism.

Shape your Career with Citi

We’re currently looking for a high caliber professional to
join our team as Vice President, Institutional Credit
Management (ICM) Product Developer - Artificial Intelligence
(AI) - Hybrid (Internal Job Title: Product Developer - C13)
based in Mumbai, India. Being part of our team means that
we’ll provide you with the resources to meet your unique
needs, empower you to make healthy decisions and manage your
financial well-being to help plan for your future.  For
instance:

- Citi provides programs and services for your physical and
  mental well-being including access to telehealth options,
  health advocates, confidential counseling and more.
  Coverage varies by country.
- Citi provides access to an array of learning and development
  resources to help broaden and deepen your skills and
  knowledge as your career progresses.
- We offer our employees resources and tools to volunteer in
  the communities in which they live and work. In 2019, Citi
  employee volunteers contributed more than 1 million
  volunteer hours around the world.

The Product Manager will join our mission to transform how
Citi manages Wholesale Credit Risk (WCR), specifically
focusing the development of a new platform utilizing
Emerging Technologies, among others Generative and
traditional AI, to manage how we make origination decisions
and perform credit monitoring.

The wholesale lending products under focus include Corporate
Lending, Leverage Finance, Real Estate, Securitization,
Treasury and Trade Solutions, and more.

As a Product Manager, you will be a part of a new
organization named Institutional Credit Management (ICM),
which was recently formed to transform the way Citi manages
the extension of wholesale credit to institutional clients.
ICM is a global group focused on re-imaging credit control
process, policies, and technology to manage the wholesale
lending process end to end. Within ICM the Underwriting
Product Management function is responsible for managing and
developing credit risk strategies and platforms as they
pertain to risk appetite management, client engagement,
origination decision making, and credit monitoring.
Additionally, the product manager is responsible for
managing requirements and development activities that will
create a user centric experience to meet the needs of
differing business lines, regions, and regulatory demands.

With the new Emerging technologies available in current
market key responsibility of ICM Product Manager is to
develop new use cases and capabilities how to utilize these
new technologies within both current and new platforms and
processes while increasing data quality, process controls
and efficiency.

If you are an individual who takes ownership, strives for
client and risk management excellence, and learns from
different perspectives to surpass stakeholder expectations,
then this is a unique leadership opportunity for you.

This role will work in collaboration with cross-functional
and cross-business stakeholders throughout the lifecycle of
the initiative. To be successful in this role, the candidate
will have previously in-depth understanding of corporate
lending and underwriting processes and has familiarity or
experience partnering with technology to drive incremental
change according to Agile software development principles.

In this role, you’re expected to perform:

Credit Risk Activities in Scope for Transformation:

- Originations & Amendments – Transformation of the deal
  management lifecycle from prospecting through
  underwriting, credit administration, and booking
  operations.
- Periodic Assessment and Client Monitoring – Transformation
  of the credit review processes including Annual Reviews
  and Problem credit management
- Portfolio Limit Setting and Management – Transformation of
  how Citi Manages Limits across Lending and Counter Party
  Credit products, including implementation of a
  comprehensive monitoring structure.
- Client Management and Servicing – Transformation of how our
  clients interact with Citi to execute on their business
  operation needs, including statements, draws, etc.
- Core Services – Transformation of the underlying foundation,
  data, and functional services that support the above
  business activities (e.g. Financial Spreading, Collateral
  Management, Covenant Monitoring, etc.)

Key Focuses:

- Partner with various ICM colleagues and risk managers to
  develop origination and credit monitoring strategies for
  managing credit risk in a timely, accurate and complete
  manner.
- Utilize modern Emerging technologies for simplification,
  automation, quality control leading to improvement in
  Efficiency of internal processes and systems.
- Launch and manage new Products, Projects and Initiatives
  utilizing Emerging technologies and ensure both technical
  and governance deliveries of these efforts.
- Effectively navigate from the strategic to the requirement
  level, enabling the design team to confidently execute
  designs informed by user needs
- Lead a set of products working groups to maintain outcome-
  based roadmaps and sequencing, review progress towards
  OKRs, and track risk dependencies and issues.
- Conduct design workshops that enable collaboration and
  conceptual solutions, while serving diverse personas
- Leverage journey-based design strategies to drive an overall
  product design process and validate prototypes into
  production, including visual design and content strategy.
- Drive digital transformation by re-engineering operational
  business processes, focusing on optimizing target state
  processes and end to end workflow orchestration with
  technology empowerment.
- Follows market, industry, and client trends to own field and
  adapts them for application Citi’s products and solutions
  platforms.

As a successful candidate, you’d ideally have the following
skills and exposure:

- Bachelor’s degree in business / equivalent or technology
  related field
- 6-10 years of relevant experience
- Expert knowledge in various software development
  methodologies, and project management concepts (e.g.,
  requirements creation, feature identification, and user
  story writing)
- Knowledge about Generative AI and Traditional AI. Key
  features, strong and weak points of these technologies and
  Experience with their implementation in existing
  processes.
- Ability to independently lead projects through strategy,
  design and implementation phases and being accountable for
  the direct financial impact of the initiatives
- Ability to present various Projects and Product to senior
  leadership in understandable and capturing way
- Basic knowledge of programming in Python is advantage
- Familiarity in a front office, middle office, or operational
  capacity supporting the credit lifecycle execution or
  related transformation efforts in wholesale lending
- Demonstrated ability to translate end user needs into
  tangible work products that technology engineers can move
  forward to develop (e.g. Process Flows, Requirements,
  Design Artifacts, Screen Mockups, etc.)
- Track record of working across multiple stakeholders and
  collaborating with various technology partners

Working at Citi is far more than just a job. A career with
us means joining a family of more than 230,000 dedicated
people from around the globe. At Citi, you’ll have the
opportunity to grow your career, give back to your community
and make a real impact.

Take the next step in your career, apply for this role at
Citi today

https://jobs.citi.com

responsibilities: Develop new AI/ML use cases and capabilities for credit risk management platforms
Partner with risk managers to develop origination and credit monitoring strategies using emerging technologies
Launch and manage new AI-powered products, projects and initiatives for credit lifecycle transformation
Lead product working groups to maintain outcome-based roadmaps and track progress towards OKRs
Conduct design workshops to create conceptual solutions for diverse personas in credit management
Drive digital transformation by re-engineering operational business processes with AI/automation
Navigate from strategic vision to requirement level to enable design execution
Follow market and industry AI trends to adapt them for Citi's products and platforms
use_cases: Credit origination and underwriting decision automation
Credit monitoring and periodic assessment of institutional clients
Portfolio limit setting and management across lending products
Financial spreading and covenant monitoring automation
Collateral management and risk appetite management
Client management and servicing workflow optimization
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is a Product Developer/Manager role focused on building
a new credit risk platform utilizing Generative AI and
traditional AI. The role involves developing new AI use
cases and capabilities, implementing AI technologies in
processes, and launching AI-powered products for origination
decisions and credit monitoring. While it's a product role,
the focus is on actively developing AI-powered solutions,
not just supporting infrastructure.
